title: [FEATURE] - Implement "decode" parameter in pull() function and add build/remove_volume function as api/image.py in docker py
body:
I'm reworking a tool using docker py in order to work with podman py. This tool has the following code section to use docker py:
```python
                    self.__client.api.pull(repository=ConstantConfig.IMAGE_NAME,
                                           tag=name,
                                           stream=True,
                                           decode=True,
                                           platform="linux/" + image.getArch()))
```
In docker-py, this `pull` function is defined [here](https://github.com/docker/docker-py/blob/a8bac8822127a8f446893a4e7e60d4b5149333bf/docker/api/image.py#L351) and it can be imported by `APIClient` lib.

podman-py has the same `APIClient` library but the related function that can do the same job of the docker one above seems to not be implemented. There is a similar one [here](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/8a4cbf3c8aaf90bfc36b5611288b9de9becca469/podman/domain/images_manager.py#L294).

Could you please implement `decode` parameter in the pull function in podman?

Is it possible to implement `api.build` and `api.remove_volume` as well?

comments.body:
Hello @inknos any news on this request? We would like to have it in order to integrate Podman py to Exegol. Thanks

EDIT: here `decode` parameter proposed PR https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/484
